title: Question about extending the auditing protocol with custom security detection
author: rocklile
body: Hi Shelby team,I'm interested in using Shelby for decentralized storage and would like to understand more about the auditing protocol.Specifically, is it possible (or planned) to extend the built-in auditing with custom real-time vulnerability scanning and integrity checks? My background is in security detection, and I'm exploring ways to add an extra layer of protection for sensitive data in Web3 environments.Any pointers to documentation, examples, or the whitepaper section on auditing would be really helpful. I keep a very low public profile for safety reasons, so I'm not looking to share code or details publicly at this stage — just trying to understand the technical possibilities first.Thanks in advance!

title: Request for AI Agent-Focused Example: Using Shelby Storage for Conversation History or MCP Integration with Perplexity/Claude
author: Munnahasan55
body:
Hi Shelby team,

I am an Early Access member in the Shelby Discord (my X is @mr_random14).

I love testing your MCP Server and SDKs because I’m a Web3 creator who builds and farms with AI agents.

The quickstart is easy to follow for basic uploads, but as someone who focuses more on AI agents than heavy coding, it would be super helpful if you could add a small example like:

“How to use Shelby storage for AI agent memory / conversation history” or “Simple integration with tools like Perplexity or Claude using MCP skills”.

This would help many creators like me get started faster.

I’m happy to test any new example and give real feedback!

Thank you so much!

title: Enhancing Media Kit Documentation for GenAI Content Creators
author: GiaPhatSDZ
body:
​Hi Shelby Team,
​I am a GenAI content creator deeply interested in the Hot Storage capabilities provided by Shelby.
​After exploring the current GitHub repository, I noticed that most of the documentation is highly developer-centric.
​To accelerate mass adoption, I suggest adding a Creator Track or simplified workflow diagrams specifically for the Shelby Media Kit.
​Providing clear steps for non-technical users to integrate Shelby high-speed retrieval into GenAI pipelines will significantly increase the project real-world utility.
​I am eager to test the Media Kit and provide more user-experience feedback as a power user.
​Best regards.

title: Suggestion: add a small WSL/Ubuntu Shelby CLI automation follow-up example
author: Mustem
body:
## Summary

A small WSL/Ubuntu Shelby CLI automation example could be useful in the quickstart flow.

## Why this may help

Quickstart is great for getting started, but some users may also benefit from a lightweight follow-up example showing how to automate repeated CLI usage.

For example:

- scheduled file generation
- timed upload attempts
- simple processed-file handling
- log-based monitoring

I built a community example here:
https://github.com/Mustem/shelby-cli-auto-uploader

## Suggestion

Consider adding a short "next step" or "automation example" section to quickstart, or linking to a community example for WSL/Ubuntu users.

title: Testing Shelby Quickstart on Windows
author: guunduul
body:
Hello Shelby team,

I tested the shelby-quickstart project on Windows.

Steps completed:
- Fork repository
- Clone repository
- Install dependencies
- Run dev mode
- Commit and push activity

Everything works well.

Looking forward to testing Shelby storage further.

title: UX feedback after testing Shelby Devnet dashboard
author: Andrey11012
body:
Hi Shelby team,

I tested the Shelby Devnet dashboard as a beginner user and wanted to share some feedback.

Environment:
- Petra Wallet
- Chrome browser
- Shelbynet

My experience:

1. Wallet connection worked smoothly.
2. Faucet interaction was successful.
3. Upload process was clear, but it would help to show a confirmation message after successful upload.
4. Blob list sometimes requires manual refresh to update.
5. For beginners, a short “step-by-step testing guide” section on the dashboard would be very helpful.

Overall the experience was positive and I’m excited to see further improvements.

Thanks for your work!

title: blob list not being displayed
author: aeemars
body:
*  The list command keeps not finding uploaded blobs even after making multiple uploads
*  From my findings, i discovered it's an indexer URL error. Dev team should look into this.
